thag17.2:1.2: his mind well-centered, absorbed with restrained resolves,
thag17.2:1.3: inwardly delighting in his conduct, his mindfulness,
thag17.2:1.4:
alone, content:
He’s called a monk.
thag17.2:2.3: With unfilled belly, with measured food,
thag17.2:3.1: Leaving four or five mouthfuls uneaten,
thag17.2:3.3: This is enough for the easeful abiding
thag17.2:4.1: And he covers himself with a proper robe
thag17.2:4.3: This is enough for the easeful abiding
thag17.2:5.2: it doesn’t rain on his knees:
thag17.2:5.3: This is enough for the easeful abiding
thag17.2:6.3: and that there’s nothing between the two:
thag17.2:6.4: With what will he be what in the world?
thag17.2:7.1: “May I never have anything to do with a person of evil desires,
thag17.2:7.4: With what will he be what in the world?
thag17.2:8.1: But one who is learned, wise,
thag17.2:8.4: Let him stand at the head.
thag17.2:9.1: One who is devoted to objectification,
thag17.2:9.4: the unexcelled rest from the yoke.
thag17.2:10.2: delighting in the path of non-objectification,
thag17.2:10.4: the unexcelled rest from the yoke.
thag17.2:11.4: that place is delightful where arahants dwell.
thag17.2:12.4: for they’re not searching for sensual pleasures.
thag17.2:13.1: Regard him as one who points out treasure, the wise one who
thag17.2:13.4: Stay with this sort of sage.
thag17.2:13.5: For the one who stays with a sage of this sort,
thag17.2:14.2: deflect you away from poor manners.
thag17.2:15.1: He, the Blessed One, the One Awakened, the One with Eyes,
thag17.2:15.3: And while the Dhamma was being taught,
thag17.2:15.4: I, desiring the Dhamma, turned an attentive ear.
thag17.2:16.1: My listening was not in vain:
thag17.2:17.1: Not for knowledge of previous lifetimes,
thag17.2:17.3: nor for knowledge of others’ minds, nor for psychic power,
thag17.2:17.4: nor for knowledge of beings dying & being reborn,
thag17.2:17.5: nor for purifying the divine ear
thag17.2:18.1: Sitting at the root of a tree,
thag17.2:18.2: with shaven head, wrapped in an outer robe,
thag17.2:19.2: the disciple of the Rightly Self-Awakened One
thag17.2:20.3: so a monk whose delusion is ended
thag17.2:20.4: doesn’t quiver— just like a mountain.
thag17.2:21.2: in constant quest of what’s pure,
thag17.2:21.4:
seems a storm cloud.
I don’t delight in death, don’t delight in living.
I await my time like a worker his wage.
thag17.2:24.2: it’s not non-death, ahead or behind.
thag17.2:24.4: Don’t let the moment pass you by.
thag17.2:25.1: As a frontier fortress is guarded
thag17.2:25.3:
you should safeguard yourselves.
Don’t let the moment pass you by.
thag17.2:25.5: Those for whom the moment is past
thag17.2:26.4: as the breeze, a leaf from a tree.
thag17.2:27.4: as the breeze, a leaf from a tree.
thag17.2:29.1: You shouldn’t trust in this way
thag17.2:29.2: some householders or those gone forth.
thag17.2:29.3: Having been good, they become bad.
thag17.2:29.4: Having been bad, they become good.
thag17.2:30.4: These, for a monk, are the five defilements.
thag17.2:31.4: whether he is honored or not.
thag17.2:32.2: having subtle insight into subtle views,
thag17.2:32.3: delighting in the ending of clinging:
thag17.2:32.4: He’s called a man of integrity.
thag17.2:34.2: the elder of great knowledge, centered,
thag17.2:36.1: The Teacher has been served by me;
thag17.2:37.3: So then, I’m about to unbind.
